title: Bridge Paper: Understanding Voice-Hearing Experiences in Non-Clinical Populations: A Literature Review.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.
**Phase context:** auditory verbal hallucination, hallucination proneness, voice hearing, inner speech, corollary discharge, source monitoring

Paper: Understanding Voice-Hearing Experiences in Non-Clinical Populations: A Literature Review.
DOI: 10.1007/s11126-025-10139-5
Abstract: Voice-hearing experiences (VHEs), commonly known as auditory verbal hallucinations, can range from mild to severe, rare to frequent, and negative to positive. There has been a significant increase in studies focusing on non-clinical voice-hearing experiences as compared with clinical groups. However, not many studies have investigated these experiences occurring solely in non-clinical populations.
